task: Three tasks: (1) Content/SEO audit — check the landing page, explore page, and tool page meta tags. Are they well-optimised? Any missing OG tags, duplicate title tags, or thin descriptions? Fix anything obvious. (2) Data quality pass — find and fix any tools that have obviously wrong categories (e.g. SaaS tools in wrong categories based on their name/description, tools with no tags at all). Run a query to find the top 20 approved tools with no tags and suggest/add appropriate tags for the most obvious ones. (3) Check the /dashboard route: does the claim-to-Pro flow work end-to-end? Read dashboard.py and check: after a user claims a tool, do they see the upgrade CTA? Is there any post-claim redirect or prompt to upgrade? If the flow is broken or missing, fix it.

assignments.content: SEO/meta tag audit for landing page, explore page, and tool detail page. Check: OG tags (og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url), Twitter card tags, title tag uniqueness (no duplicates), meta description quality and length (150-160 chars), structured data/JSON-LD presence. Fix anything obviously missing or thin. Files: src/indiestack/routes/landing.py, explore.py, tool.py (and components.py for shared shell).
assignments.backend: Two sub-tasks: (A) Data quality — run a query to find the top 20 approved tools with zero tags. For each, suggest and apply appropriate tags based on name/description/category. Also find tools with obviously wrong categories (e.g. auth tools in 'databases', email tools in 'payments') — fix the most egregious mismatches. After any DB changes, rebuild FTS index: INSERT INTO tools_fts(tools_fts) VALUES('rebuild'). (B) Dashboard claim-to-Pro flow — read src/indiestack/routes/dashboard.py: after a user claims a tool, do they see an upgrade CTA for Maker Pro? Is there a post-claim redirect or prompt? If the flow is broken or missing, implement it so claimed tool owners are nudged to upgrade.
assignments.frontend: Review the dashboard UI (src/indiestack/routes/dashboard.py) for the post-claim state: once a tool is claimed, does the UI show a visible upgrade CTA (Maker Pro $49/mo) in the claimed tools section? Check the HTML rendered for claimed tools — if the upgrade prompt is absent or visually weak, add/improve it. Coordinate with backend on what state/data is available.

decomposition.reasoning: Content SEO audit is fully independent and can run in parallel with backend data/dashboard work. Frontend dashboard UI work depends on backend first confirming what the claim-to-Pro flow looks like and what data is available, so it runs after. Backend handles both the data quality pass and the dashboard logic audit together since they both involve DB and Python route code.

strategy_review.reasoning: The dashboard task is split across backend AND frontend, but both would edit the same file: src/indiestack/routes/dashboard.py. IndieStack routes combine Python logic + HTML templates in a single .py file — there's no clean backend/frontend boundary. Two agents editing dashboard.py simultaneously will produce merge conflicts or one agent overwriting the other's work. Additionally, backend is overloaded with two unrelated sub-tasks (data quality + dashboard flow) while the data quality task needs explicit guidance on whether changes happen locally or on production via SSH (absolute paths only — gotcha).

approved_tasks.content: SEO/meta tag audit for landing page, explore page, and tool detail page. Check: OG tags (og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url), Twitter card tags, title tag uniqueness (no duplicates), meta description quality and length (150-160 chars), structured data/JSON-LD presence. Fix anything obviously missing or thin. Files: src/indiestack/routes/landing.py, explore.py, tool.py (and components.py for shared shell).
approved_tasks.backend: Data quality pass ONLY. Run queries against the LOCAL database to find: (A) top 20 approved tools with zero tags — suggest and apply appropriate tags based on name/description/category, (B) tools with obviously wrong categories — fix the most egregious mismatches. IMPORTANT: Do NOT use LIKE '%keyword%' for category matching — it catches substrings (gotcha). Use explicit slug lists or exact word matching. After all local DB changes, rebuild FTS: INSERT INTO tools_fts(tools_fts) VALUES('rebuild'). Note: these are LOCAL changes only — they'll need a separate production data fix later via SSH with absolute paths.
approved_tasks.frontend: FULL dashboard claim-to-Pro audit — you own this entirely, both logic and UI. Read src/indiestack/routes/dashboard.py end-to-end. Check: (1) After a user claims a tool, what state/data is available? (2) Does the claimed-tool section show a visible Maker Pro upgrade CTA ($49/mo)? (3) Is there a post-claim redirect or prompt nudging upgrade? (4) If the flow is broken, missing, or visually weak — implement/fix it. This is the revenue path from claimed tools to paid subscriptions, so make the CTA prominent but not aggressive. Do NOT gate features behind payment — just nudge (vision.md: never degrade the free experience).

conditions: Dashboard work goes to frontend ONLY — backend must not touch dashboard.py
Backend must not use LIKE '%keyword%' for finding miscategorized tools — use explicit checks or tag-based matching
Backend data quality changes are LOCAL only this run — production DB fixes need a separate SSH step with FTS rebuild
Frontend should read vision.md and pricing context before implementing upgrade CTAs — the nudge must be soft, not a gate
risk_flags: Original plan had two agents editing dashboard.py — guaranteed conflict
Backend LIKE substring matching gotcha is relevant for finding miscategorized tools
If backend accidentally runs data changes on production without FTS rebuild, search serves stale results
strategy_review.alternative: Cleaner split: Content owns SEO audit (read-heavy, low risk). Backend owns data quality only (DB queries + fixes, no dashboard). Frontend owns the entire dashboard claim-to-Pro flow (logic + UI + CTA in one pass). This eliminates the file conflict and gives each agent a focused, independent workstream.
